member.name: Andrew Western

addresses: DWP Ministerial Correspondence Team, Caxton House, Tothill Street, London, SW1H 9NA

member.synopsis: Andrew Western is the Labour MP for Stretford and Urmston , and has been an MP continually since 15 December 2022. He currently holds the Government post of Parliamentary Under-Secretary (Department for Work and Pensions).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Confucius Institutes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Deputy Foreign Secretary, whether he has (a) received representations on and (b) made an assessment of the potential relationship between (i) Confucius Institutes and (ii) the propaganda department of the Chinese Communist Party.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministers are briefed regularly and in detail on matters relating to China. They are clear that as with all international bodies operating in the UK, Confucius Institutes must operate transparently and within the law, and with a full commitment to our val...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Confucius Institutes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she has discussions with (a) Cabinet colleagues and (b) universities on the future of Confucius Institutes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government takes seriously any concerns about overseas interference in the UK’s higher education (HE) sector, including through Confucius Institutes, and regularly assesses the risks facing academia. The department has taken action to remove any direc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Jimmy Lai
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Deputy Foreign Secretary, whether UK consular officials (a) have been granted consular access to Jimmy Lai and (b) are able to visit him in prison.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministers and officials continue to raise Jimmy Lai's case at the highest levels with the Chinese and Hong Kong authorities and have been clear in our calls for his release, while continuing to press for consular access. Our repeated requests for consular...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Confucius Institutes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she has made an assessment with Cabinet colleagues of the potential national security implications of Confucius Institutes on UK university campuses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Professions: Prescriptions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps her Department is taking to ensure that the prescribing responsibilities of (a) dietitians and (b) other allied health professionals are regularly (i) reviewed and (ii) updated to reflec
writtenQuestions.answerText: Dieticians can currently supply and administer medicines using Patient Group Directions and train to use supplementary prescribing. At present, these are deemed to be the most appropriate medicines mechanisms for their use.The University of Surrey is carr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Dietetics: Prescriptions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether officials in her Department have had discussions with (a) the British Dietetic Association and (b) other relevant stakeholders on the potential extension of independent prescribing responsi
writtenQuestions.answerText: Dieticians can currently supply and administer medicines using Patient Group Directions and train to use supplementary prescribing. At present, these are deemed to be the most appropriate medicines mechanisms for their use.The University of Surrey is carr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Dietetics: Prescriptions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of commissioning research on the potential impact on patient care of granting independent prescribing responsibilities to dietitians.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Dieticians can currently supply and administer medicines using Patient Group Directions and train to use supplementary prescribing. At present, these are deemed to be the most appropriate medicines mechanisms for their use.The University of Surrey is carr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Dietetics: Prescriptions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of extending independent prescribing responsibilities to dietitians.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Dieticians can currently supply and administer medicines using Patient Group Directions and train to use supplementary prescribing. At present, these are deemed to be the most appropriate medicines mechanisms for their use.The University of Surrey is carr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: Domestic Abuse
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what proportion of claimants applying for the Domestic Violence Easement extend the initial unevidenced four week easement to (a) 13 and (b) 26 weeks.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested is not readily available and to provide it would incur disproportionate cost.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Rented Housing: Deposits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities, whether his Department has made recent progress on introducing a lifetime deposit for renters.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in our 2022 White Paper ‘A Fairer Private Rented Sector’, the market has responded to the ambition of our manifesto commitment for a ‘lifetime deposit’.We continue to monitor the development of commercial lifetime deposit solutions, alongside k...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities

writtenQuestions.heading: Bounce Back Loan Scheme: Repayments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps her Department is taking to recover repayments of Bounce Back Loans under £50,000.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Lenders are responsible for the recovery of these loans in line with the standards and legal obligations set out under the schemes. The Department and the British Business Bank continue to work closely with UK Finance and accredited scheme lenders to supp...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Healthy Start Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps her Department has taken to restore the availability of data on Healthy Start uptake on the Healthy Start website.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Work and Pensions has identified an error in their data that means that the data published for Healthy Start uptake from July 2023 onwards was incorrect. It is important to state that the data used to calculate the uptake rate is not us...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Healthy Start Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps her Department has taken to restore the availability of data on the uptake of Healthy Start vouchers on the Healthy Start website.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Work and Pensions has identified an error in their data that means that the data published for Healthy Start uptake from July 2023 onwards was incorrect. It is important to state that the data used to calculate the uptake rate is not us...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: First Time Buyers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities, what estimate he has made of the number of first time buyers in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DLUHC publishes estimates of first-time buyer numbers as part of the English Housing Survey. See Chapter 3 and Annex 3.2 in: English Housing Survey2022 to 2023: headline report - GOV.UK.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities

writtenQuestions.heading: Leasehold: Reform
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities, with reference to legal advice given to the Law Commission in December 2019, whether his Department has received any recent advice on the compatability of the marriage value element
writtenQuestions.answerText: Our reforms seek to make it cheaper and easier for leaseholders to extend their lease or purchase their freehold. An Impact Assessment for the Leasehold and Freehold Reform Bill has been carried out and published on the Parliament website at: Leasehold an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities

writtenQuestions.heading: Leasehold: Reform
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities, what estimate he has made of the potential cost to businesses of proposals to abolish the marriage value element of leasehold enfranchisement; and whether he has made an assessment o
writtenQuestions.answerText: Our reforms seek to make it cheaper and easier for leaseholders to extend their lease or purchase their freehold. An Impact Assessment for the Leasehold and Freehold Reform Bill has been carried out and published on the Parliament website at: Leasehold an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities

writtenQuestions.heading: AI Safety Summit
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, if she will publish the names of people who attended the AI Summit 2023 on behalf of (a) organisations and (b) governments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The names of the organisations and governments who confirmed their attendance prior to the AI Safety Summit was previously published here: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/ai-safety-summit-introduction/ai-safety-summit-confirmed-governments-and-...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Sector: Access
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, if he will make it her policy to introduce funding for public bodies to ensure that they are able to provide offline options for all service users.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that everyone has affordable access to public services, whether online or offline. Government departments are already required by the Government's Service Standard to provide support via alternative channels for all...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Sector: Access
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, if he will make it her policy to ensure that everyone has the choice to access public services offline by phone, letter or face-to-face.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that everyone has affordable access to public services, whether online or offline. Government departments are already required by the Government's Service Standard to provide support via alternative channels for all...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Digital Technology
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps she is taking to ensure that the Digital Inclusion Strategy takes account of people who are unable to get online.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring no-one is left behind in this digital age. The 2022 UK Digital Strategy outlined work that will promote digital inclusion and help people to get online, including committing to at least 85% gigabit broadband coverag...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
